items.notes: The question word for origin. Кайдансыз? means 'Where are you from?' in polite register. Кайдансың? is the informal equivalent.
The word for father. With the possessive suffix it becomes атам (my father). Ата is also used as a respectful address to an older man.
The word for mother. With the possessive suffix it becomes апам (my mother). Апа is also a warm address for an older woman.
Term for an older brother. Also used as a respectful address to any young-to-middle-aged man who is older than you. Байкем means 'my older brother'.
Term for an older sister. Also used as a polite address to any woman who is older than you but not yet elderly. Эжем means 'my older sister'.
Means both daughter and girl depending on context. Менин кызым means 'my daughter'. The ы is a back unrounded vowel, close to a muffled 'i'.
The common word for family as a household unit. Both vowels are front rounded (ü and ö). Туугандар is used for the wider circle of relatives.
The genitive form of мен (I). In Kyrgyz the noun after менин also takes a possessive suffix: Менин атам (my father). Both markers together is standard and not redundant.
A very common profession word. Менин апам мугалим — My mother is a teacher. No suffix needed on the predicate noun in a simple identity statement.
The capital of Kyrgyzstan. Useful in origin statements: Мен Бишкектенмин — I am from Bishkek. After Бишкек, the ablative suffix is -тен (not -ден) because к is voiceless.
The plural form for relatives. The root is тууган (kinsperson, born of). Туугандарым means 'my relatives'. Used when speaking about the wider family rather than the immediate household.
Kyrgyz has one third-person pronoun for all genders. Ал менин байкем means both 'He is my older brother' and 'She is my older sister' depending on context.
